text.title: Frontend Design Variant Exploration Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules grounding the Frontend Design Variant Exploration methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Phase 0 MUST capture type + style adjectives + tech constraints in a one-page brief BEFORE brainstorming. Skipping the brief is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a brief, variants drift everywhere; with one, divergence is bounded by intent.

rule.statement: Each variant MUST differ on ≥3 axes from every other variant (typeface, density, color, motion, layout grammar). Palette-only swaps are rejected as 'variants'.
rule.rationale: Exploration value comes from genuine difference; near-duplicates waste the exercise.

rule.statement: Each variant ships with a 3–5 sentence rationale linking its choices back to the brief. Variants without rationale are rejected.
rule.rationale: Selection decisions need explicit comparison surface.

rule.statement: Variants MUST be working code (runnable in Storybook), not screenshots / Figma mocks.
rule.rationale: LLM-generated mocks lie about what builds; working code does not.

rule.statement: Phase 3 MUST record the user's selection + rationale. Implicit selection ('we just used the first one') is rejected.
Phase 4 MUST emit Storybook stories + typed component(s). Variants left as one-off prototypes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without handoff, the exercise is design theatre.

rule.statement: Each variant lives in `designs/variant-N-<slug>/` with its own README + rationale. Mixed-into-src variants are rejected.
rule.rationale: exploration code must not pollute production paths.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Frontend Design Variant Exploration under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
